author: Amy Lane Waterman
description: Join Betsey Bobbitt, a girl with an undeniable sweet tooth, as she discovers new recipes for candy and sweets to make with the reader. An entertaining cookbook with a narrative! (Summary by Mary Kay)

title: Female American
author: Unca Eliza Winkfield
description: “The Female American; Or The Adventures of Unca Eliza Winkfield, Written by Herself in Two Volumes” was anonymously published under the pseudonym Unca Eliza Winkfield in 1767. Unca Eliza, a half-Native American and half-English woman, is shipwrecked on an island. Discovering the remnants of past civilization, Unca Eliza utilizes the tools she finds and her extensive knowledge (derived from her experience with both the English and Native Americans) to survive the island and immanent encounters with indigenous peoples. This audiobook is a collaborative project by graduate students in the English Department at the University of Oregon. - Summary by Kathleen Gekiere

title: Moll Flanders - Version 2
author: Daniel Defoe
description: This warm and witty novel presents itself as the first person narration of an immensely likeable and resourceful woman who, born in the most shameful of circumstances, comes to be desired by, and indeed married to, many men, in the course of her life — some of whom she inveigles into marriage on false pretences — who, when all else fails, resorts to an extraordinarily successful life of crime, pulling many ingenious heists until at last she is nabbed and faced with a real prospect of being hanged. Moll's descriptions of the amorous inclinations of males are often very funny — and not the least part of the joke here, of course, is that Moll's voice itself is actually the creation of a male. (Summary by Peter Dann)

title: Utopia (Burnet translation)
author: Thomas More
description: This book is all about the fictional country called Utopia. It is a country with an ‘ideal’ form of communism, in which everything really does belong to everybody, everyone does the work they want to, and everyone is alright with that. This country uses gold for chamber pots and prison chains, pearls and diamonds for children’s playthings, and requires that a man and a woman see each other exactly as they are, naked, before getting married. This book gave the word 'utopia' the meaning of a perfect society, while the Greek word actually means ‘no place’. Enjoy listening to this story about a country that really is too good to be true. (Summary by Jenilee.) This is the 17th century translation by Gilbert Burnet, edited in the 19th century by Henry Morley.

title: Wisdom of the Ancients, A Series of Mythological Fables
author: Sir Francis Bacon
description: "Now I suppose most people will think I am but entertaining myself with a toy, and using much the same kind of licence in expounding the poets’ fables which the poets themselves did in inventing them; and it is true that if I had a mind to vary and relieve my severer studies with some such exercise of pleasure for my own or my reader’s recreation, I might very fairly indulge in it. But that is not my meaning. Not but that I know very well what pliant stuff fable is made of, how freely it will follow any way you please to draw it, and how easily with a little dexterity and discourse of wit meanings which it was never meant to bear may be plausibly put upon it. Neither have I forgotten that there has been old abuse of the thing in practice; that many, wishing only to gain the sanction and reverence of antiquity for doctrines and inventions of their own, have tried to twist the fables of the poets into that sense; and that this is neither a modern vanity nor a rare one, but old of standing and frequent in use; that Chrysippus long ago, interpreting the oldest poets after the manner of an interpreter of dreams, made them out to be Stoics; and that the Alchemists more absurdly still have discovered in the pleasant and sportive fictions of the transformation of bodies, allusion to experiments of the furnace." - Summary from Bacon's Preface

title: Gargantua and Pantagruel, Book V
author: François Rabelais
description: The Life of Gargantua and of Pantagruel (in French, La vie de Gargantua et de Pantagruel) is a connected series of five novels written in the 16th century by François Rabelais. It is the story of two giants, a father (Gargantua) and his son (Pantagruel) and their adventures, written in an amusing, extravagant, satirical vein. There is much crudity and scatological humor as well as a large amount of violence. Long lists of vulgar insults fill several chapters. - Summary by Wikipedia

title: Anchored & Sent with Ken Burns
author: Ken Burns
description: Anchored & Sent is a podcast about finding God in everyday life. Through stories, lessons, and reflections from faith, family, work, and everyday experiences, Ken Burns explores what it looks like to follow Jesus wherever God has planted you. Anchored in Christ. Sent into the world.

title: Erec and Enide
author: Chrétien de Troyes
description: A medieval romance in which Erec goes through many trials until he is sure of Enide’s loyalty and true love (Summary by M. J. Boyle)

title: Manon Lescaut
author: Abbé Prévost
description: Published in 1731, Manon Lescaut (on which the Puccini opera is based) takes as its themes passionate, tragic love, and redemption through suffering. It is the story of the Chevalier des Grieux, a student, who sees Manon as she is being taken to a convent, and instantly falls in love with her. He offers to save her from the convent, and the two young lovers run away to Paris. There follow many adventures and tribulations, throughout which the Chevalier remains steadfastly loyal to his love. summary by Mary Bard

title: Yarn of Old Harbour Town
author: William Clark Russell
description: This novel opens in 1805, during the Napoleonic Wars between England and France. A retired sailor, Captain Acton, reminisces about his time at sea now he lives on shore and wonders about a ship seen burning at sea the previous night. But there is one more voyage for the Captain, this time taking his daughter Lucy. As all sailors know, a woman on board brings bad luck.

title: Beverly Wilshire Aesthetics Beverly Hills Medical Spa
author: Tyler The Skin Savant, Dr. Behnoush Zarrini
description:
Welcome to the audio destination of Beverly Wilshire Aesthetics—The Maison of Supreme Skin™ in Beverly Hills. Hosted by Forbes-recognized, dual board-certified physician Dr. Behnoush Zarrini, this masterclass series bridges the gap between surgical-grade anatomical science and the art of subtle luxury refinement. Moving past the surface-level templates of standard medical spas, we explore the biological blueprints behind ageless expression, progressive cellular optimization, and long-term dermal integrity. From the mechanics of Botox longevity to the advanced architecture of the Zarrini Lift™, step inside clinical certainty and discover an elite approach to facial rejuvenation where your expression is softened, never silenced. Deep dives, cellular data, and the science of inevitable results.
Explore more at https://www.bwaesthetics.com/medical-spa-beverly-hills/

title: Futility: A Novel on Russian Themes
author: William Alexander Gerhardi
description: From the preface written by Edith Wharton: Then I fell upon Futility. Some one said: “It's another new novel about Russia” –and every one of my eager feelers curled up in a tight knot of refusal. But . . . I read on, amused, moved, absorbed, till the tale and the journey ended together. This, it seems to me, is the most striking quality of Mr. Gerhardi's book: that he has (even in this, his first venture) enough of the true novelist's “objectivity” to focus the two so utterly alien races to whom he belongs almost equally, by birth and bringing-up – the English and the Russian – to sympathize with both, and to depict them for us as they see each other, with the play of their mutual reactions illuminating and animating them all. - Summary from the preface by Edith Wharton

title: Jacob's Room (version 2)
author: Virginia Woolf
description: Virginia Woolf’s third novel lacks a conventional narrative style and some say even a plot. It follows Jacob from his childhood, through his education at Cambridge and finally to his death in World War I. The prose repeatedly shifts its point of view and the reader is challenged to find connections between the narrative fragments. Largely from the comments of others we come to know the sequence and some moments of Jacob’s life but we never fully learn who Jacob is. The literary experimentation in Jacob’s Room is used even more successfully in Woolf’s later novels. (Summary by DaveC)

title: Tales of the Uneasy
author: Violet Hunt
description: Nine twisty-turny tales of tragic human drama, played out in Victorian parlors, death beds and lonely country roads. This collection of Violet Hunt writings has all the requirements for short story entertainment: flirtatious beauties, mismatched love, ''lung symptoms'', and cruel, cruel fate. Sometimes the horror has a ghostly source, but often the horror is rooted in our very human pathology. - Lisa Reichert

title: Prince Zaleski: Master of Deduction
author: M. P. Shiel
description: In 'Prince Zaleski,' M. P. Shiel introduces a detective like no other. Nestled in his lavish, antique-filled residence, Zaleski navigates a world of mystery and intrigue. Surrounded by relics from the past, including a mummy and ancient tomes of forgotten knowledge, he tackles perplexing cases that leave others stumped. Combining sharp logical reasoning with a deep understanding of history and lore, Zaleski uncovers truths hidden in the shadows of time. As he deciphers clues and confronts the bizarre, listeners will be drawn into a realm where the ordinary meets the extraordinary. This audiobook blends elements of detective fiction with supernatural undertones, offering a unique listening experience that challenges the mind and captivates the imagination. Discover how Zaleski's peculiar methods and keen insights unveil mysteries that lie just beneath the surface of reality. Join him on a journey filled with twists, turns, and the unexpected, and experience the thrill of solving crimes shrouded in the arcane.

title: Season to Taste.
author: Ariane Frydman
description:
This is a food podcast about cooking by feel.
Each week, I talk through one of my recipes the way I actually make it, no strict measurements, no perfection. 
What I was craving, what was happening in my life, and why that dish makes sense right now.
Along the way, I connect it to food trends, internet culture, and the quiet shift back to cooking at home. 
This isn’t about cooking like a chef. It’s about trusting your taste, paying attention, and making something that feels good.
Come sit with me in the kitchen and season to taste.

title: BugOff Lincoln | Pest Control Tips & Home Protection
author: Charlie Thomas
description:
Welcome to BugOff Lincoln | Pest Control Tips & Home Protection, the podcast dedicated to helping homeowners and businesses keep their properties pest-free. Each episode shares practical advice on preventing and controlling common pests, including ants, termites, rodents, spiders, mosquitoes, cockroaches, and more.
Whether you're looking for seasonal pest prevention tips, home maintenance advice, or expert insights from the pest control industry, this podcast provides simple and actionable information to protect your home and family.
If you need professional Pest Control Lincoln NE services, visit https://bugofflincoln.net/ (https://bugofflincoln.net/) to learn more about BugOff Lincoln's trusted pest control solutions for homes and businesses throughout Lincoln, Nebraska.
Hosted on Ausha. See ausha.co/privacy-policy for more information.

title: Life on the Line
author: Pro-Life Union
description:
Life on the Line with Chris Pushaw

"Life on the Line" covers the week in the pro-life world in the Delaware Valley. It weaves in news, events and prayer to capture the pulse of the Culture of Life in Philadelphia. We also feature guests from over 25 related organizations to give an informed and comprehensive picture of where things stand in terms of neonatal care, support, and counseling, public advocacy and educational outreach. We welcome guests from all backgrounds and political perspectives for the purpose of reasoned and respectful debate on what is admittedly the "third rail" of American cultural discourse.

Christopher Pushaw serves as the President of the Pro-Life Union of Greater Philadelphia. Prior to that he ran the primary campaign for Mark Houck in the First Congressional District of Pennsylvania. Chris comes to the Pro-Life Union with an extensive background in pro-life and legal advocacy. He had served as Executive Director and General Counsel for the Pennsylvania Pro-Life Federation, the largest of its kind in the Commonwealth and the official state affiliate for the National Right to Life Committee. In that capacity, he coordinated legislative and educational outreach with respect to pro-life issues as well as grassroots activity throughout the state. Chris got started in area pro-life outreach as a volunteer speaker for and later President of Pennsylvanians for Human Life, using his legal background to trace the genesis of Roe and its legal, ethical and cultural ramifications in schools throughout the Delaware Valley. He also represented children in Family Court in Philadelphia through the Child Advocacy clinic and was involved in the Big Brothers program.

Chris has been a practicing “Philadelphia Lawyer” for twenty-five years. Chris earned his undergraduate degree at The Catholic University of America, a master’s degree from the University of Wisconsin (where he attended a talk given by Sarah Weddington, the lawyer who represented “Jane Roe”) and finally Yale Law School, where he wrote a major research paper on genetic screening and partial birth abortion.

title: Podcast about  Atlantis
author: Katerin Lujanni Ulloa
description: Hello everyone, and welcome to our podcast. My name is Liz, and today I am here with my classmates Katherin and Yuhao. In this episode, we are going to explore one of history’s greatest unsolved mysteries: Atlantis.

title: Broken Healthcare
author: Ray Kober
description:
“Broken Healthcare” isn’t just another podcast exposing flaws in the healthcare system—it’s a movement. Hosted by the entertaining and knowledgeable Ray Kober, we pull back the curtain on the hidden forces driving up costs and making care confusing. Whether you’re an employer, an industry insider, or just someone tired of overpriced, low-quality care, we arm you with the insights to fight back. Get real stories, expert insights, and actionable solutions to take control of healthcare decisions—because better choices start with better information.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Come Unprepared
author: Zat
description: Come Unprepared is a conversation with no rehearsal. Each guest arrives with a topic. The host hears it for the first time at recording. What follows is an unscripted interrogation of the idea — where it comes from, what it reveals, and whether it holds.

title: Drop bears - the italian edition
author: Omny
description: The best drop bears content

title: From My Father Podcast with Tangie Callahan
author: Bridge Church Media
description:
Welcome to From My Father with Tangie Callahan.

Take your shoes off + stay awhile.

Grab a cup of coffee—or a matcha—and join Tangie for honest conversations about faith, life, healing, purpose + everything in between. Through real stories, real questions + biblical truth, you’ll discover the Father’s heart for every season.

Whether you’re navigating a difficult season, searching for answers, growing in your faith, or simply longing for more of God, this is a place where you can pull up a chair, be encouraged + be reminded that you’re not walking alone.

New episodes feature heartfelt conversations, practical wisdom, personal experiences + the timeless truth of God’s Word—all designed to point you back to the Father.

Real life. Real questions. The Father’s heart.

The conversations begin THIS AUGUST. 🤍

title: The Little Linguist
author: Salima
description:
Step into an exciting world where stories come to life in three different languages! Journey with us into a space where the beauty of English, French, and Arabic is shared through a blend of beloved fables, uplifting affirmations, and the everyday expressions that make a house a home.

Whether you are protecting your family’s heritage roots or planting brand new ones for the very first time, The Little Linguist helps children and parents alike grasp the gift of language in a way that is both fun and unforgettable.

We believe that knowing another language isn't just a skill, it’s a life-changing superpower that opens doors to the world.

title: The River Branch Podcast
author: River Branch
description: We seek to grow and mature in Messiah, gaining a deeper understanding of His way, His truth, and His life. This includes both the foundation of the Faith and His sacrifice for His people. We value His pattern of discipleship and repentance so that we may learn to walk out our faith together. We do not represent any particular religious denomination, movement, or political affiliation. Learn more at www.riverbranchfellowship.com

title: Couch Kings Podcast
author: Couch Kings Podcast
description: Hear ye, hear ye! The couch kings doth descend from their thrones to give you their terrible movie takes.

title: The Wonderer’s Podcast
author: Ian’s Tracks
description:
The Wonderers Podcast shines light into the life stories of creative entrepreneurs.

Beautiful, honest conversations about life, work, creativity, art, adventures, hopes & dreams.

You can find out more about the Wonderer's Podcast at www.ians-tracks.co.uk/thewondererspodcast

Want to get in contact? E-mail Thewondererspodcast@hotmail.com

Follow us on Instagram @the_wonderers_podcast

About Ian

Ian is a singer songwriter, frequent traveller and founder of his unique music and travel blog Ian's Tracks. He quit a corporate career to travel the world and make music. Ian writes songs for TV and film, inspired by the incredible people and places from his adventures.

title: The Grace Between
author: Tony Rice Recovery Center
description:
Stories of recovery, resilience, faith, and the grace that meets us between where we've been and where we're going.


What happens between addiction and recovery. 

Between brokenness and healing. 

Between who we were and who we're becoming. 

Between struggle and strength. 

Between rock bottom and purpose. 

Between grace received and grace given.


Welcome to The Grace Between, hosted by Sam Hillis, a Licensed Counselor, Clinical Director, Community Engagement Coordinator, and person in long-term recovery. With 11 years of personal recovery and 10 years of experience working in the field of addiction treatment, Sam brings both professional expertise and lived experience to honest conversations about healing, growth, and the realities of rebuilding a life.


Each month, you'll hear authentic stories of recovery, resilience, faith, and personal transformation from individuals who have overcome adversity and found hope on the other side. Featuring conversations with people in recovery, community leaders, counselors, advocates, and changemakers, this podcast explores the challenges, victories, and lessons learned along the journey.


Whether you're seeking encouragement, resources, practical insights, or simply a reminder that lasting change is possible, you'll find meaningful conversations that inspire hope and strengthen community.

The Grace Between is proudly sponsored by and recorded at the Tony Rice Recovery Center, where lives have been transformed through recovery, support, and second chances for more than three decades.


Because recovery isn't just about getting sober—it's about discovering purpose, building connection, strengthening faith, and creating a life worth living.

title: 100cuci Decoded
author: 100cucipro.com
description: Welcome to the 100cuci Podcast — your trusted source for responsible online entertainment, platform compliance, and digital gaming culture across Southeast Asia. Each episode explores key topics including fair play standards, bonus policies, payment security, player wellbeing tools, and the latest technology shaping the industry. Whether you're a new or experienced player, we help you make smarter, safer decisions. Hosted by the 100cuci team in Shah Alam, Malaysia. Visit us at https://100cucipro.com/

title: The Complete Picture
author: The Complete Picture Podcast
description:
The Complete Picture is a podcast for film completionists - seasoned obsessives and curious newcomers alike. 
Each month, host Neil Rogers is joined by a special guest to dive deep into the full filmography of one noteworthy (and sometimes notorious) filmmaker. Together, they rank every release, debating the highs, lows, and everything in between. 
The Complete Picture is a Film in Dublin podcast.

title: Just Breathe Podcast
author: Fotini Markou
description: In this podcast, you will learn about many different aspects of psychology, such as its interaction with many parts of life and many career options.

title: Look! Squirrel!
author: Garry.Ozols
description: Look! Squirrel! is the podcast for people who think in tangents — and aren't sorry about it. Host Garry Ozols follows whatever's caught his attention this week: a fascinating person worth interviewing, a topic that deserves a deeper dive, a social issue that needs calling out, or something that just wandered into his brain and refused to leave. No fixed format. No single genre. Just genuine curiosity, honest conversation, and the occasional sharp left turn. If your mind has ever started one thought and finished somewhere completely different — pull up a chair. You're among friends.

title: Break Up With Corporate
author: Debbie Mitten (Virtually Debbie)
description:
Are you a woman ready to escape the corporate grind and build a business on your own terms? "Break Up With Corporate", hosted by Debbie Mitten, is a fun and informative podcast that delivers practical tips, strategies, and real conversations about the journey from leaving toxic corporate cultures to launching your own successful business. She definitely keeps it real.

Debbie Mitten is a Business Mentor for women and owner of Virtually Debbie, LLC.
She has 37 years of corporate experience and lived the journey of transitioning from a burned out corporate employee, finding herself laid off at 59 with no backup plan. While healing from burnout, she started her own business at the age of 60 and is dedicated to helping other women make this transition while they still have their jobs and time to prepare for the next chapter.


https://BreakUpWithCorporate.com

title: Savage Body Sanctuary
author: Ross Snyder
description: Everything fitness, spiritual, and fun in the wilderness.

title: The Shadow Of The Wolf: A Dr. Thorndyke Mystery
author: R. Austin Freeman
description: In 'Shadow of the Wolf,' Dr. John Thorndyke finds himself entangled in a web of love, betrayal, and murder. When tensions rise between Mr. Purcell, his wife Maggie, and Mr. Varney, a long-time business partner, the stakes become deadly. As emotions flare and secrets unravel, Thorndyke must rely on his unique blend of medical knowledge and investigative skills to uncover the truth behind a shocking crime. This gripping detective tale immerses listeners in a world where every clue counts and nothing is as it seems. Experience the thrill of the chase alongside Thorndyke as he navigates a complex love triangle that leads to unexpected consequences. With rich character development and a riveting plot, R. Austin Freeman crafts a mystery that will keep you guessing until the very end. Dive into this classic detective story that blends science and sleuthing, perfect for fans of timeless whodunits and intricate plots.

title: Life With Max
author: Max
description:
Life With Max

Welcome! I'm Max Marcoux.

For more than 27 years, I've had the privilege of serving as a pastor, helping people discover the hope, peace, and purpose that come from following Jesus. Along the way I've been blessed with an incredible wife of over 30 years, raised four children, owned a fitness gym, coached people through life's challenges, and worked with individuals living courageously with Parkinson's disease.


Life has taken me on some amazing adventures—from racing motorcycles and playing in rock bands to studying music at Berklee College of Music, traveling, creating music, and exploring the beauty of God's creation through photography. Each experience has taught me something about faith, perseverance, and the incredible grace of God.


One truth has become clear to me:

Life isn't transformed overnight. It's renewed one step, one choice, and one day at a time.

That's what this podcast is all about.


Each episode offers practical, Bible-centered encouragement to help you grow closer to God, understand His Word, strengthen your faith, build healthier relationships, and live with greater purpose, hope, and joy. We'll talk about real life, real challenges, and the timeless truth of Scripture, discovering together how following Jesus transforms every part of our lives.


Whether you're just beginning your faith journey or you've been walking with Jesus for many years, my hope is that you'll leave every episode encouraged, challenged, and better equipped to become the person God created you to be.

Thanks for being here.

Let's renew our lives—one step at a time.

title: What if it All Works Out?
author: Daviana Sankey
description:
Life is an ever evolving journey that you and I often overthink, but what if it all works out?
Join me as I navigate life hosting authentic conversations for the over thinker turned overcomer.
New episodes premieres Wednesdays
Season 2 premieres July 8, 2026!

title: Sakhi
author: Emcure Pharmaceuticals Limited
description: Sakhi, Emcure’s women‑centric patient awareness initiative, focuses on sharing key insights that help women adopt the right lifestyle behaviours for a healthier tomorrow. Through its engaging podcast, Sakhi guides women on how to care for their health across every life stage, highlighting essential imperatives to consider throughout their wellness journey. By fostering awareness and encouraging sustainable lifestyle choices, Sakhi empowers women to build long‑term well‑being and lead healthier, more informed lives.

title: The Idiot's Guide to Soccer Tactics and Regulations
author: Trevor
description: The show outlines a radical soccer strategy called the Pragmatic System, which prioritizes winning over aesthetic appeal. The argument is that teams should reject the "beautiful game" and instead, for example, use long balls to bypass risky passing in their own defensive zones. Players are instructed to take hard, central shots aimed at the goalkeeper to force chaotic rebounds and defensive errors. The guide emphasizes administrative alignment, requiring coaches to secure board protection against being fired for using these "ugly" but effective tactics.

title: ちょっと占ってく？
author: ＠CBCラジオ
description:
「恋愛も仕事も人間関係も、なんだかうまくいかない。」
そんな時は、もりママにちょっと相談してみませんか？


占い師・若杉守人（もりママ）が、リスナーの皆さんから寄せられたお悩みに占いの視点から寄り添うPodcast番組。
恋愛、仕事、人間関係、自分自身のこと――。
日々のモヤモヤや迷いに、もりママがやさしく向き合います。

進行はフリーアナウンサーの石坂美咲。

未来を決めつけるのではなく、自分らしく前に進むためのヒントをお届けします。

迷ったら、またここにちょっと占いに来てくださいね。


占い師・若杉守人（もりママ）

名古屋中区新栄で占いバーLaboLasu・尾張旭市三郷駅で占い屋LaboLasuを営む、知る人ぞ知る名古屋の人気占い師。

主な占術は霊視。その人の写真を見ることで占うことができる。


フリーアナウンサー・石坂美咲

CBCラジオレポートドライバーやNHKレポーターなどを経て、CBCラジオ＃プラスのパーソナリティを務めるなど、フリーアナウンサーとして活動中。

男の子のママとして育児奮闘中。実は占い好き。

﻿

▼お悩み募集中！
番組では皆さんからの相談メールを募集しています。
恋愛、仕事、人間関係など、どんなお悩みでも大歓迎です。

https://form.hicbc.com/rcmc/8df5f9088163

毎週金曜日19:00新エピソード公開。

title: Introduction to The Real S.H.I.T Podcast
author: therealshitpod
description: An introduction to Real Stuff Happening In Transition. A platform for honest radical discussion based in today’s reality.

title: True Fan, True Opinions
author: Sue
description:
Being a fan isn't always easy -and sometimes the truth needs to be said.


True Fan, True Opinions is a sports podcast hosted by Sue, a passionate lifelong fan who believes being a true fan doesn't mean blindly agreeing with everything your team does. With a special focus on Canadian teams and athletes -but never limited to them - Sue delivers honest, no-nonsense opinions on the biggest stories, biggest moments, and biggest frustrations across the world of sports.

When teams deserve praise, they'll get it. When they deserve criticism, they'll get that too. 


Because being a true fan means telling the truth.

title: Project Tyros
author: Stir the Plot
description: Stir the Plot Studios presents Project Tyros: an actual play using the Hunter: the Reckoning system with some homebrewing. This story follows a group of graduate students working under their private university's mysterious organization to learn to hunt the supernatural. With parties to attend and monsters to hunt, studying tends to sit on the backburner for these four.

title: Pink Hats
author: Briana Therrien
description:
Welcome to Pink hats! Where the game is on, but we're barely paying attention. We're talking games, gossip, relationships, red flags, internet drama, and everything happening on and off the field. So grab your drink and a snack for game time!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

author: Keith
description: Tech and Software Development

title: The Obligation Show
author: Jason Murphy
description:
Can you hear me now?” 🎤 
​After a three-year silence, it’s time to break it. As the lyrics go, ”Take the fire from the sun, but I’ll never hear the warning,” so we’re turning up the volume to face the truth head-on. There’s no more running when you’re ”silent running on the burning sea”—it’s time to speak.
​The Obligation Show returns this Friday, June 25th.

title: A Tweet Too Far
author: HorneyMedia AndPublishing
description:
Lucy Connolly, a mother and childminder, witnessed in horror the southport attacks in July 2024.

She then took to Twitter to vent her fury.

In the next ten days, she was arrested and later sent to prison for 31 months.

She was released on 21 August 2025.

Jailed for a tweet...this is her story.


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Growth Unfiltered Podcast
author: Glory Josephine
description:
Self-esteem is the foundation of confidence, resilience, and personal growth. The Power of Self-Esteem is a podcast dedicated to helping you recognize your worth, overcome self-doubt, and build the confidence to pursue your dreams without fear.

Each episode explores practical strategies for developing a healthy self-image, breaking free from negative thinking, setting healthy boundaries, and embracing your unique strengths. Through inspiring stories, expert insights, and actionable advice, you'll discover how strong self-esteem can positively impact your relationships, career, mental well-being, and overall happiness.

Whether you're recovering from setbacks, seeking greater confidence, or simply striving to become the best version of yourself, this podcast will encourage and equip you every step of the way.

Join us as we unlock the mindset that transforms lives—because when you believe in yourself, there's no limit to what you can achieve.

title: Ai technologies update and renovations
author: Artificial Tech Creations
description: Technology enthusiast passionate about innovation, artificial intelligence, and digital transformation. Driven by curiosity and creativity, I enjoy exploring emerging technologies and turning ideas into practical solutions that make a positive impact.

title: Back Road Bandit
author: Team Phoenix Media
description: From the back roads of Oklahoma to stories from across America, Back Road Bandit covers rural life, road trips, delivery adventures, automotive myths, current events, small-town stories, and whatever crazy thing shows up along the way. Real talk, no fluff, and plenty of laughs.

title: Superposed
author: Aidan Lewis
description:
Every decision exists in superposition — multiple outcomes, all possible, until you choose. But how do you know which decision to make? 
Superposed applies quantum cognition — an emerging framework that uses quantum mechanics principles to explain how humans make decisions — to history's most consequential choices. 
Learn from this deeper perspective on how political, business, and scientific leaders have made the decisions that effect our everyday lives to gain a sharper mental model for the decisions you face today.

title: Unfiltered Pride
author: Ash
description: Unfiltered Pride is a fearless LGBTQ+ podcast sharing real stories, raw experiences, and unapologetic truths. From coming out and transitions to surgeries, relationships, and self-discovery, we amplify queer voices — no filters, no fear, no shame. Tune in and hear the stories you won’t hear anywhere else.

title: 📚 Inventive Storyteller | Brand Legends in 90 Seconds
description: Inventive Storyteller uncovers the surprising stories behind the world's most recognizable brands. In each fast-paced episode, Devin Miller explores the origins, pivots, branding decisions, and defining moments that transformed ordinary companies into household names. Perfect for entrepreneurs, founders, marketers, and anyone fascinated by business history.

title: Siwasnac - English AI Notebooks for convenience 英文AI筆記
author: Siwasnac
description:

title: It’s Just BMX
author: Dozer and Stew
description: A BMX racing podcast. Interviews, Industry news, Race reviews, Plenty of laughs and nothing too serious with your hosts Dozer and Big Stew.

title: Weatherford Daze
author: Jason Weatherford
description: Welcome to Weatherford Daze! We’re Jason and Rachel Weatherford, a husband and wife who love great books, good movies, and real conversations. On this channel, we’ll be chatting about the stories that inspire us, sharing what life is like as a special needs family, and opening up about our faith.

title: STILL FIGURING IT OUT
author: Shreena Chandarana
description:
Life doesn’t come with a roadmap, and honestly, I don’t have all the answers.
This is a space for the conversations we don’t always have out loud—the messy, uncomfortable, beautiful parts of growing up, navigating relationships, heartbreak, family expectations, culture, therapy, self-discovery, and everything in between.
Still Figuring It Out is a reminder that none of us have life completely figured out. We’re all learning, growing, healing, and becoming different versions of ourselves as we go. 
We’re all still figuring it out :)

title: The Night Land - A Journey Beyond the Last Redoubt
description:
The Night Land by William Hope Hodgson is one of the most extraordinary works of early science fiction and cosmic horror ever written. Set in an unimaginably distant future where the sun has long since died, humanity survives inside a colossal metal pyramid known as the Last Redoubt, surrounded by an endless landscape of darkness, volcanic plains, and ancient terrors. Beyond its protective walls, colossal supernatural beings known as the Watchers observe in silence, while unknown horrors roam the Night Land, creating an atmosphere unlike any other classic novel.

Blending epic adventure, haunting romance, philosophical reflection, and dark fantasy, The Night Land inspired generations of writers, including H. P. Lovecraft and many modern creators of cosmic horror. Its unforgettable world-building, immense scale, and eerie sense of isolation make it a landmark of speculative fiction and one of the most ambitious public domain classics available today.

Whether you're a fan of dystopian fiction, gothic horror, post-apocalyptic worlds, or timeless science fiction, this audiobook offers an unforgettable journey into one of literature's most mysterious and imaginative universes. Experience William Hope Hodgson's visionary masterpiece in immersive audio and discover why The Night Land continues to fascinate readers and listeners more than a century after its publication. Summary by Dream Audiobooks

title: Angielski na co dzień – krótkie lekcje, które działają
author: EDU ENGLISH OK
description: Podcast dla osób, które chcą rozwijać swój angielski poprzez słuchanie. W odcinkach pojawiają się różne tematy, dzięki którym możesz poznawać nowe słownictwo, naturalne zwroty oraz sposób, w jaki język angielski funkcjonuje w codziennej komunikacji. To spokojna i przystępna forma nauki, która pomaga osłuchać się z językiem i stopniowo budować pewność w jego rozumieniu. Jeśli chcesz uczyć się angielskiego regularnie i w naturalny sposób, ten podcast jest dla Ciebie.

title: The Byrdcast
author: Hilary Holladay
description: The Byrdcast features interviews with newsmakers and other interesting people in and around Orange County, Va. It's a companion to Byrd Street, a Substack newsletter covering the news in Orange County.

title: GA4-240202501-AA1-EV02 Audio
author: Edinson Jose Gutierrez Fontalv
description: This podcast was created as evidence for the English learning activity GA4-240202501-AA1-EV02. It includes personal information, attitudes, beliefs and obligations in academic and work contexts.

title: Dear Recovery
author: csteddy12
description: Hello beautiful people, this podcast is a listener supported space for anyone struggling with eating disorders, disordered eating, body distress/dysmorphia, or Co-occurring mental health and substance use challenges. Each episode is guided by listener emails and DM’s, questions stories, fears, and hopes read and responded to with compassion clinical insight and lived recovery and experience hosted by, me Colleen, a marriage and family therapist working in private practice as well as a person in long term recovery from an eating disorder and substance use this podcast is not about perfection or quick fixes. It is about being heard, being understood and knowing you’re not alone. This space is inclusive of all genders, bodies, and identities, including members of the LGBTQIA plus community. Whether you’re in active struggle, early recovery long term recovery or simply searching for language for what you’re experiencing, you are welcome here. This podcast is not a substitute for therapy or medical care, but it is a place to sit, listen and remember that recovery in all the imperfect forms is possible. Just a short disclaimer for this podcast; I do not discuss any numbers or weights or explicit behaviors, and I will always keep your identity anonymous if that is your wish. Most importantly, I always encourage you to seek out higher levels of professional care as appropriate, and I will answer emails or DMS to questions and experiences, etcetera where I feel I can genuinely give advice or share my own personal experience, and perhaps what helped me. A note to remember is that recovery in all its imperfect forms is possible and I truly just want to connect with you, help, hear you, and hopefully answer some of those anxiety provoking questions that I know I was riddled with for so many years. I promise, no question is too stupid, outlandish, bizarre or weird, because I know how isolating this disease is, but I promise you are not alone and any topic/concern is welcome here :) Email me at csteddy12@gmail.com and put in the subject line ”Dear Recovery” or DM at @dear_recovery1. Cant wait to hear from you!

title: Your Priestess Permission Slip
author: Tenae Stewart
description: A 5-day challenge to prepare for your priestess path

title: Beyond the Inputs
author: Clay Hills Ag
description: Welcome to Beyond the Inputs, where agriculture conversations go beyond products and sales pitches. Join farmers, agronomists, and industry leaders from across the U.S. as we discuss crop inputs, humics, soil health, new technologies, and the practices shaping modern agriculture. Our conversations are casual, honest, and practical—like sitting around a table with friends after a long day in the field or gathering at the local café. No selling, just real-world experiences, fresh ideas, and insights to help farmers navigate the future of agriculture.

title: The Sixth Age: A Medieval History Podcast
author: Mason Hughes
description:
The Sixth Age: A Medieval History Podcast

Forget everything you think you know about the "Dark Ages." The Sixth Age is a deep dive into Medieval History that strips away modern myths to reveal the world as it was actually lived.

Why did people in the Middle Ages see the world the way they did? From the fall of Rome to the dawn of the Renaissance, we reconstruct the Medieval worldview using primary sources and period-accurate perspectives. If you’re a fan of European history, historiography, and immersive storytelling, join us as we reject later stereotypes to rediscover the vibrant, complex, and often surprising lives of our ancestors.

Subscribe to The Sixth Age for a fresh, authentic look at the history of the Medieval world.

title: The Outlet | Podcast
author: The Roseburg Plug
description:
🎙️ The Outlet Podcast
Real conversations with the people building community.
Hosted by Shane Hayes, The Outlet Podcast shines a light on the small business owners, creators, nonprofits, organizers, and everyday people making a difference across Douglas County and beyond.
This isn’t surface-level promotion — it’s about the human side of the journey. Why people started, what they’ve overcome, what they believe, and what keeps them going.
Honest, local, inspiring, and sometimes unexpected.
Because behind every business, brand, or mission… there’s a real story worth hearing.

title: Talk Rodeo To Me Podcast
author: talkrodeotome
description: We are a rodeo news outlet committed to keeping rodeo alive for the next generation. Here you'll find interviews with rodeo athletes, coaches, stock contractors and those within the rodeo community

title: For Every Birth, For Every Baby, For Every Family by Fletchers Solicitors
author: Fletchers Solicitors
description:
For Every Birth. For Every Baby. For Every Family. is a podcast dedicated to improving maternity, neonatal and perinatal care across the UK through open, thoughtful and honest conversations.

Hosted by the Birth Injury team at Fletchers Solicitors, the series brings together families, legal experts, clinicians, charities, campaigners and professionals working across maternity and birth injury care to explore the realities faced when things go wrong - and what can be learned to help create safer care for every family.

Through personal stories, expert insight and collaborative discussion, the podcast aims to give families a voice, raise awareness of the lasting impact of birth injuries, and encourage constructive conversations around accountability, learning and improvement within UK maternity services.

While grounded in the legal perspective and the experiences of our clients affected by birth injury, this podcast is focused on something bigger: helping build understanding, supporting positive change, and contributing to a safer future for babies, parents and healthcare professionals alike.

Each episode explores the human impact behind the headlines, the importance of support and rehabilitation, and the shared goal of improving outcomes for families across the UK.

Follow us now to make sure you don't miss an episode.

title: There's No Way
author: TeamFolk
description:
Hosted by husband-and-wife team Lance and Pastor Kat Folk, There's No Way is a weekly conversation that unpacks the biblical text behind Sunday's sermon with humor, curiosity, and just enough button-pushing to keep things interesting.

Published each Thursday, the podcast invites listeners into the conversation before Sunday to learn more about the text or afterward to continue wrestling with the text. Along the way, Lance asks the questions many listeners are likely wondering, while Kat explores the Scripture, its historical context, and what it might mean for our lives today.

Episodes generally follow the Revised Common Lectionary and the liturgical calendar of the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America (ELCA), including festivals, commemorations, and other special observances. Kat serves as pastor of Celebration of Grace Lutheran Church in Canton, Georgia, and The Church Lot, a Lutheran campus ministry at Kennesaw State University.

(Sometimes we cuss a little)

title: Once Upon A Mother Fuckin Time ...THE END
author: Author MS Woodley
description: Not here to tell you what to think . Frameworks that work

title: CONVERSASIANS: The Podcast
author: RepresentASIAN Project®
description:
CONVERSASIANS is a video and podcast series from RepresentASIAN Project that brings the Asian diaspora’s stories to life through honest, thoughtful conversation.
Hosted by RepresentASIAN Project founder and therapist Madelyn Chung, each episode invites a notable Asian voice to reflect on who they are, where they come from and what they carry.
More than a spotlight series, CONVERSASIANS is a space for the stories, questions and connections that shape us, offering fresh perspectives on identity and representation.

title: You Represent Science
author: Deb Newitz
description:
Medicine has authorities. It has institutions, journals, guidelines, and gatekeepers. What it doesn't always have is YOU.

You Represent Science is hosted by Dr. Deb Newitz — a doctor, a patient advocate, and someone who spent years inside a system she eventually had to start questioning. This show is built on a simple but meaningful idea: that patients represent science. They are part of it. Their experiences, their stories, and their outcomes matter.

Each episode examines the gap between what gets published, what gets prescribed, and what patients actually live. This is where that examination begins.

title: Breaking The Loop
author: Leanne Khan
description:
*Breaking the Loop* is a raw, honest podcast about noticing the patterns that quietly run our lives and choosing to interrupt them. Instead of offering quick fixes or polished self-help, it explores what it actually feels like to catch yourself in destructive habits, question your thinking, and consciously reset.
In collaboration with Hush.FM, the conversations move between everyday struggles and bigger philosophical ideas, helping listeners understand how their minds have been shaped by experience, culture, and routine.
Some of the recurring themes include:
* *The psychology behind change* – Looking at rituals and habits as practical tools that help reach the subconscious, where lasting change really begins.
* *Reclaiming identity* – Exploring South African Coloured heritage, the pain of undocumented history, and what it means to be the generation responsible for changing the story.
* *Radical accountability* – Challenging excuses and self-deception by focusing on the one thing we can control: how we show up every day.
* *Finding light in difficult seasons* – Using South Africa's realities, like load shedding, as a metaphor for staying grounded and moving forward when motivation, certainty, or external support disappear.
At its core, *Breaking the Loop* is for people who feel different, who question inherited beliefs, and who are trying to build a better future without a clear blueprint. It's a conversation for anyone carrying the weight of the past while choosing not to be defined by it.

title: LK's podcast
author: Lehlogonon Mashao
description: Two kids one is a boy and one is a girl they want to swap ganders

title: High Energy Judaism
author: Rabbi Mendy Levertov & Gilad Bogner
description: High Energy Judaism explores Jewish fundamentals through the lens of Chassidus and Kabbalah. Using the weekly Torah portion as it's launching point, it aims to give basic Judaism in clear everyday language. If you're looking to reconnect to your roots, this is a great place to start. Hosted by Rabbi Mendy Levertov and Gilad Bogner.

title: Glow Girl Insight
author: Tochi .U.
description:
Glow Girl Insight is Tochi.U’s candid podcast unpacking the real side of modern womanhood in the digital age.
From viral beauty trends and online culture to dating dilemmas, burnout, and everyday pressures, Tochi delivers sharp commentary, personal stories, and honest conversations that actually hit home.
Real talk with warmth and wit — no fluff, no filters.

title: Insights With Deven
author: Deven
description:
“Insights with Deven” is a podcast where successful entrepreneurs, investors, creators, and industry leaders share the real stories behind their journey. From building businesses and creating wealth to overcoming struggles and taking risks, each episode delivers practical insights, mindset shifts, and life lessons that inspire growth.
Hosted by Deven, this show explores business, money, property investing, entrepreneurship, immigrant success stories, and the mindset required to create an extraordinary life.

title: Decoded Genius
author: Decoded Genius
description: Decoded Genius distills decades of mastery from the top 0.0001% in every field. From health and longevity to business, AI, and psychology, each episode helps ambitious people shortcut the learning curve and level up their health, wealth, and happiness.

title: Tangle and Gaze: Jumping Spider Queens
author: Dr. Hakeem Ali-Bocas Alexander
description:
TANGLE & GAZE

Two jumping spiders. One man paying close attention. A daily unfolding of small wonders that most people walk right past.

Dr. Hakeem Ali-Bocas Alexander brings you into the quiet, curious world of his two arachnid companions — Tangle, a regal jumper with a calm, watchful presence, and Gaze, a bold jumping spider whose chelicerae flash blue-green iridescence.

Each episode is a window into their enclosures, their hunts, their habits, and the unexpected bond that forms when you stop long enough to observe two tiny predators living alongside you. The show moves between spider care, personal reflection, and the strange beauty of sharing space with creatures who demand nothing and notice everything.

Listeners join a conversation that began with a spider clinging to a car hood at highway speed and grew into a commitment to name, house, feed, and understand two eight-legged lives. Along the way, the podcast explores what it means to name something — and the quiet responsibility that comes with that act.

New episodes arrive as the spiders eat, molt, exp

title: AG10: Covering 10 Lessons from 10 Years of Business
author: Hosted by Bilal Kaiser, Founder and Principal at Agency Guacamole
description:
Agency Guacamole, a leading independent PR firm, is celebrating its 10-year anniversary this year with a new podcast series. Bilal sits down with beauty and lifestyle industry leaders across marketing and communications to talk about business, life and everything in between.
Each interview will reveal the guest’s defining moments, challenges and lessons over the past decade.
Listeners will gain valuable insights into how industry leaders got started, what drives them today and how their perspectives have evolved throughout their careers. 
Visit us @agencyguacamole or agencyguacamole.com.

title: Edward'sTime of Departure: A Time Travel Story
author: Edward Carr
description:
A reading of the novel "Time of Departure: A Time Travel Story" by Edward Carr. 
 
Includes special features, interviews, and an
 exciting dive into the behind the story!

title: Voice From the Cave: A Penny Parker Mystery
author: Mildred A. Wirt Benson
description: When Penny Parker and her father take a vacation, they expect relaxation and fun. Instead, they find themselves drawn into a web of intrigue involving foreign spies. Alongside a mysterious woman, they uncover secrets that lead them deep into danger. As Penny navigates unexpected twists, her curiosity and courage are put to the test. With each clue they discover, the stakes rise, and the line between friend and foe blurs. This thrilling detective fiction takes listeners on a suspenseful journey filled with unexpected encounters and clever deductions. Join Penny as she solves the mystery lurking in the shadows, proving that adventure can strike in the most unlikely places.

title: The Twenty-Six Clues: A Murder in the Crime Museum
author: Isabel Ostrander
description: In a private crime museum, Mr. Calvin Norwood stumbles upon a shocking scene: a murdered woman displayed under a Mexican blanket. The guests—an eclectic mix of a retired police officer, a fireman, an investigative professor, and a blind secretary—find themselves drawn into a web of secrets and intrigue. As they navigate their own connections and confront hidden motives, the tension rises. Inspector Druet and his team join the investigation, but with so many suspects and unexpected twists, who can be trusted? 'Twenty-Six Clues' immerses listeners in a gripping detective story where every character holds a piece of the puzzle. Journey through the clues, confront the suspects, and uncover the truth in this thrilling tale of mystery and deception. With a blend of suspense and rich character dynamics, this audiobook promises to keep you guessing until the very end.

title: Mystery Potato
author: hoche
description: Welcome to Mystery Potato, where the world's greatest mysteries come to life. From ancient civilizations and forgotten empires to archaeological discoveries and unexplained historical events, we explore the stories, secrets, and mysteries that shaped human history. Join us as we uncover the past—one mystery at a time.

title: The Trill Sanctum
author: DroCole
description:
The Trill Sanctum Podcast is where purpose meets truth. Hosted by DroCole, each episode explores authenticity, mindset, personal growth, entrepreneurship, faith, conscious living, and self mastery to help you unlock your next level and full potential and live with intention.
Whether you're building a business, strengthening your character, or searching for deeper meaning, these conversations are designed to challenge your thinking and inspire purposeful action.

If you find value in the show, consider joining the Supporters Club. Your support helps keep The Trill Sanctum independent while giving you access to exclusive content, early releases, and ad free listening.
Join the Supporters Club: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-trill-sanctum--7134761/support

title: Unfiltered: 2BLKWomen Podcast
author: Unfiltered 2BLKWomen
description:
What started as Coffee & Conversations is now something bolder, louder, and realer. No sugarcoating. No holding back. Just honest conversations, real perspectives, and unapologetic voices.
🎙️ Welcome to Unfiltered: 2 Black Women with Dana James and Lya Williams

title: The Roman Hat Mystery: A New York Murder Case
author: Ellery Queen
description: In the heart of New York City, a theater stage turns into a crime scene when a notorious lawyer is found dead from poison during a packed performance. What makes this case even more puzzling is the empty seats surrounding the body and the disappearance of his top hat. Inspector Richard Queen and his son, Ellery, a clever mystery writer, dive into the investigation, unraveling secrets that intertwine the world of theater with dark motives. As they sift through the clues and confront a cast of intriguing characters, they face the challenge of revealing the truth before the curtain falls. This is the gripping first installment in the Ellery Queen series, where every twist invites you to play detective alongside the Queens. Join the mystery that will keep you guessing until the very end.

title: The Words We Don't Say
author: The Words We Don't Say Podcast
description: The Words We Don't Say explores the silences that keep us stuck—in battlefields, firehouses, and therapy rooms. Join us as we sit with practitioners, healers, advocates, and people who've "been there, done that" for real talk and real connection around what it means to serve... And what it means to be human. What goes unspoken in your day-to-day life?

title: The Breathitt Podcast
author: Breahitt Middle School STLP
description: The Breathitt Bobcast is created by students, for the community, this podcast shines a spotlight on the voices, stories, and moments that shape life in Breathitt County. From school events and student achievements to local history and community happenings, each episode brings listeners closer to the people and experiences that make our home unique. Whether we’re interviewing classmates, highlighting local leaders, or covering events that matter, this is your inside look at what’s happening in our school and throughout the county.

title: Sabbath School with Branch Davidians
author: Mary Zebrowski
description: We present or expound on a principle or belief related to the SDA Sabbath School quarterly each week. We invite you to visit our website www.bdsda.com to learn more about who we are and, just as important, who we are not. Tune in each week for new and interesting insights to your Sabbath School studies.

title: Speaker School
author: Alex Demczak
description:
Speaker School is a podcast designed to help listeners pursue their dream of becoming a professional speaker. Hosted by author and entrepreneur Alex Demczak, Speaker School teaches you to unleash your speaking potential to become a confident, influential communicator. Whether you're aiming to book your first paid speaking engagement, climbing the corporate ladder, or just want to level up your speaking game, Speaker School is here to be your supportive partner on this exciting journey of becoming a dynamic and impactful speaker.
Alex Demczak is a full-time speaker who travels the country to perform over 50 speaking engagements per year. In this podcast, Alex will give you first-hand experiential advice on how to start and grow your speaking business.

title: History with Alex
author: Alex
description: Welcome to History with Alex! We explore the greatest moments in human history, from ancient civilizations and legendary battles to fascinating archaeological discoveries. I talk about historical events I definitely wasn't there to witness and archaeological finds I definitely didn't uncover myself. If you love history, mysteries of the past, and the stories that shaped our world, you're in the right place. New episodes every week.

title: Beyond the Stack
author: Bold Media Group
description: Beyond the Stack is a tech podcast for people who want to go deeper than buzzwords. We talk AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity, software development, automation, and all the weird future-tech stuff that’s starting to become real.

title: The Popcorn Bucket
author: ctrew
description: "I'll bring the reviews & roasts. You bring the popcorn." Welcome to The Popcorn Bucket, where your host, Carter, will dive into movies and tv shows and give his honest reviews and opinions.  From ranking franchises, to talking smack about different movies.  The Popcorn Bucket has it all.  See you at the theater with popcorn in hand!

title: Grow Further
author: Ashley fedinard
description: Welcome to our growing further podcast show were we discover the key rules for starting and managing a side hustle including legal consideration time management conflict of interest and maintaining professionalism while balancing your main job

title: Criminalist Behavior Podcast-A Louisiana True Crime Podcast
author: Criminalist Behavior Podcast
description: Welcome to Criminalist Behavior-A Louisiana True Crime Podcast, the podcast that takes you deep into the heart of Louisiana’s history and current, most chilling, true crime stories. I’m your host, Sarah Soudelier, and I’ll be guiding you through the darkness of these crimes and all those involved. Each week, we dig deep, on those forgotten crimes or those crimes currently happening with the undying legends that continue to haunt us. So lean in, listen, and get ready because true crime is anything but ordinary. Stay with me as we unravel what happened in these mysteries.

title: Best Of Bananas For Business
author: Podgo
description: Bananas For Business helps businesses turn their customers into fans and those Fans into their biggest marketers. Welcome to the Bananas For Business podcast, hosted by Jared Orton, where we help you create exceptional customer experiences to build loyal and raving fans. After more than a decade of learning what frustrates fans about the baseball industry, Fans First Entertainment has truly flipped the script for what fans expect when they come to a Savannah Bananas game at Grayson Stadium. The Fans First team has been featured on MSNBC, Entrepreneur Magazine, ESPN, and more for the captivating, sold-out baseball circus experience in Savannah, GA. Putting the mantra “Fans First. Entertain Always.” into action, the Savannah Bananas have become experts in providing remarkable customer experiences and are a prime example for companies looking to turn their customers into fans. By strategically identifying friction points in their customers’ average experiences, the Bananas have learned to stop doing what their customers hate and go all-in on intentionally mapping memorable experiences for their fans. Fans First Entertainment focuses on their core values of the Fans First Way to Always Being Caring, Different, Enthusiastic, Fun, Growing and Hungry. Fans First Entertainment and the Savannah Bananas realized money was being wasted on marketing and that creating an experience where the customers do the marketing for you could pay dividends. The Fans First Experience podcast will give you the road map on how to implement the Fans First Experience in your business and create fans for life. The approach is simple. Whatever is Normal, do the exact opposite. Fans First Entertainment exists to bring a Fans First mentality to the world. Jared, team owners Jesse and Emily Cole, and the rest of the team do that by entertaining hundreds of thousands of fans with the world-famous baseball circus, The Savannah Bananas, and by teaching other businesses the Fans First Experience. Love your people more than you love your customers and love your customers more than you love your product. Bananas For Business doesn’t just work for a crazy baseball team. This process has been shared with Banks, Real Estate teams, Service-Based Businesses, Media Publishers, Home Builders, Accountants, B2B teams, HVAC, Event Planners, Non-profits, Freelancers, Schools, Car Dealerships, Churches, Medical Groups and more. When businesses begin to re-imagine the customer experience, transformation can happen in any industry. People are tired of being advertised to and will be influenced more by an amazing experience over traditional marketing. People are starving for great experiences and businesses can truly set themselves apart from their industry by winning on experience. A little bit better product at a little bit cheaper price no longer is enough to set businesses apart from everyone else. Customer experience will be the determining factor for most customers when it comes to deciding on a business. People will be willing to spend more and tell others about your business if you provide them a remarkable experience. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to learn more about how to create raving fans for life! Welcome to Bananas For Business.

title: Lean and Confident Podcast
author: Jake and Joe Sharp
description: Real conversations about fitness, mental health, and overeating—so you can get lean without hating yourself while dealing with real life.

title: Reset Medical and Wellness Center Podcast
author: Reset Medical and Wellness
description:
Welcome to the Reset Medical and Wellness Center Podcast—where real stories meet real solutions for mental health, trauma recovery, and total wellness.
We go beyond surface-level advice to explore how stress, anxiety, PTSD, and burnout impact the nervous system—and what you can do about it.
Featuring experts, patients, and real-world experiences, we cover:
– The hidden effects of trauma
– Why traditional treatments don’t always work
– Breakthrough approaches like NSR (Neuro-Stellate Reset)
– Real recovery stories and results

title: The Maritime Forecast Podcast
author: Develop Consulting
description: Maritime Forecast explores the future of UK shipbuilding and maritime operations. Hosted Develop Consulting, in partnership with the Society of Maritime Industries (SMI). We bring together industry leaders to discuss defence procurement, operational excellence, supply chain sovereignty and how UK yards can compete globally. Essential listening for shipyard operators, maritime manufacturers, supply chain managers and anyone passionate about British maritime. Practical insight from people who understand complex manufacturing and what it takes to transform operations.

title: The Abundance Mandate
author: The Engineer
description: This is a 4 episode podcast that discusses the effects of AI and Robotics on the future, especially the future of work. Episode one lays out the problem with some historical perspective, while episode 2 and 3 suggest and then detail a three pillar solution and episode 4 makes it real with actual numbers and how we might work the national budget to mitigate the issues.

title: Advantage Arjun
author: Arjun Goel
description: This is a fun, lighthearted, podcast run by a pre-teen boy. He loves sports and he will be sharing that love with you.

title: Maleeha Shahid Speaks: Conversations on Belonging and Public Life
author: maleehashahidspeaks
description: Maleeha Shahid Speaks is a reflective podcast on belonging, leadership, and public life. Each episode draws from books I’m reading, stories I’m living, and conversations shaping our communities. I explore themes of identity, resilience, integrity, and compassion, while also sharing insights from my own writing and upcoming book. This is a space for honest reflection, thoughtful dialogue, and stories that help us better understand ourselves and the world around us.

title: Seri33 Talks
description: Welcome to the Seri33 Podcast — exploring responsible digital entertainment, player protection, and transparency in Malaysia’s online gaming landscape. This podcast is for informational purposes only; please follow local regulations and practice responsible engagement — learn more at https://seri33my.com/

title: Saturday Night Sermons
author: Acts 1:8 Church
description: Saturday Night Sermons features weekly biblical teaching from Acts 1:8 Church, a Missional Outpost of Crossroads Bible Church. Join Pastor Jason and occasional guest preachers as they open God's Word with Christ-centered messages that encourage faith, deepen biblical understanding, and equip believers to live on mission. Whether you're part of our church family or listening from afar, these sermons are here to help you grow in your walk with Christ.

title: 250 Years of Servitude
author: Black Veterans Project
description: Created in reflection of America’s 250th anniversary, "250 Years of Servitude" is a limited-series podcast that brings together perspectives from the four founders of Black Veterans Project (BVP)—tracing their journeys from military service to reparative justice and exploring what’s at stake for our multiracial democracy today.

title: For Cindy
author: Niusha Tabibi Gilani
description:
This podcast is a series of audio letters to Cindy, an English-speaking friend, through whom Iranian culture, history, memory, and everyday life are introduced to listeners who may know little about Iran.

Each episode explores one aspect of the Iranian world: rituals, food, music, literature, history, beliefs, suffering, joy, and the many ways Iranians have created meaning, resilience, and beauty.

This is not a formal history lesson, nor a touristic introduction to Iran. It is a personal and human attempt to tell intimate, engaging stories from a culture that carries thousands of years of memory, art, grief, faith, humor, and life.

Through these letters to Cindy, each episode becomes a bridge between Iran and the English-speaking listener — a bridge made of voice, music, storytelling, and memory.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Faith Like My Father
author: Lexie and Tim
description: Faith Like My Father is a father-daughter podcast about faith, life, and the conversations that shape us along the way. Together, we talk about Scripture, everyday struggles, questions we don't always have answers to, and what it looks like to follow Jesus through different seasons of life. Whether you've been in church your whole life or you're simply curious about faith, we hope these conversations feel like pulling up a chair and joining us at the table.

title: Someone Had to Ask
author: Amy Vujaklija
description: In Someone Had to Ask, I ask questions about life’s complex systems that most people didn’t know they could ask.

title: Rude Gyal Summer
author: Lamar Rowe
description:
Rude Gyal Summer is a mixtape where smooth R&B emotions meet the energy of Dancehall culture. From late-night love songs and passionate melodies to infectious island rhythms and unforgettable vibes, this project captures the highs and lows of romance, desire, and self-expression. Whether you’re winding down after a long day, cruising through the city at night, or sharing a moment with someone special, Rude Gyal Summer delivers a soundtrack filled with soul, rhythm, and Caribbean flavor.


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Pediatric Urology Academy
author: Amin Afrasiabi, MD, Pediatric Urologist
description: Pediatric Urology Academy is an evidence-based educational podcast hosted by Amin Afrasiabi, MD, Pediatric Urologist. Each episode explores pediatric urology through clinical cases, landmark studies, practical decision-making, surgical principles, and emerging research. Topics include posterior urethral valves, vesicoureteral reflux, hydronephrosis, UPJO, megaureter, neurogenic bladder, hypospadias, disorders of sexual development, pediatric stone disease, and reconstructive surgery. Designed for pediatric urologists, urologists, residents, fellows, and healthcare professionals worldwide.

title: Building confidence Avenue
author: Joyce Matipha
description: Building confidence involve developing a strong belief in your ability through positive thinking setting achievable goals, facing challenges and learning from experience.

title: What Remains
author: Pondd Suwitcha Sugthana
description:
A serialized cosmic-horror story set in the Backrooms. Kai is the best scout left in the endless yellow rooms beneath reality — and father to a boy who has never seen the sky. When the things that hunt the dark suddenly start running from something worse, the last safe place humanity has left may not stay safe for long.
New chapters, narrated in full. Literary, slow-burn dread — atmosphere over jump-scares, closer to The Magnus Archives and NoSleep than to gore. Best with headphones, lights off.

title: Virtuous Athlete
author: Catholic Athletes for Christ - Mike Hoendorf
description: Host Mike Hoendorf interviews athletes, coaches, priests, and other sports loving people about the intersection of sports in their Catholic faith.

title: Black Coffee + Boundaries
author: Ashley
description: Black coffee is straightforward and wakes you up — just like a good boundary. Ashley enters motherhood and takes you with her for the ride. Also a stepmom, a self-care enthusiast, and a lover of thrifting she shares the thrilling, the nerve wracking, and the mundane moments of motherhood as she experiences it all for the first time.

title: The Spike Podcast
author: Spike Art Magazine
description: The Spike Podcast makes sense of the chaos of contemporary aesthetic culture, from one of art and art criticism’s leading independent magazines. Expect pop, theory, critique, and the insights of madness from artists, writers, and other arts practitioners with strong views about the present and its place in history – we even promise to leave the arguments in.

description: Prolly gonna be good

title: Authentically Human
author: itsadrienneleigh
description: A space for honest conversations about life, alignment, and what it means to intentionally create a life you actually enjoy living.

title: The Thoughtful Therapist
author: The Thoughtful Therapist
description: The Thoughtful Therapist is hosted by Jimmy Sclater, a therapist and speaker who has spent his career sitting with people as they wrestle with life’s deepest questions, raw joys, pain, clarity, and confusion. Drawing on doctoral work in how we develop our idea of self and years of listening to human stories, Jimmy explores the questions that shape us: who we are, the choices we make and why, and how we think about life. No quick fixes. No lectures. Just careful thinking, shared honestly. If you're ready to think bigger about what matters, pull up a chair. New episodes when the ideas demand it.

title: Twisted Kracker
author: Steven Hensel
description: A podcast for Natives to sound off and get a little Twisted about Native issues.

title: Regulate First: Quick Returns
author: Heath Kruebbe
description:
Short guided practices to help you pause, reset, and return.
Whether you're feeling overwhelmed, distracted, exhausted, or simply disconnected, each Quick Return offers a simple way back to yourself—in just a minute or two.
Listen before a meeting.
After a difficult conversation.
During a busy day.
Or anytime you need a moment to reconnect.
One breath.
One moment.
One return.
Inward first, then forward.

title: The Iron Table
author: Tristan van Eck
description: The Iron Table is a space for honest conversations about faith, growth, and what it means to become a man of God. Around the table, we share real stories, lessons, and struggles from our own journeys as we explore discipleship, purpose, leadership, and character. It’s about growing together, asking deeper questions, and having the conversations men don’t always have enough of.
